title: Spring Boot
objectives: Build a REST controller + JPA service quickly

sections.markdown: Spring Boot is an opinionated framework on top of Spring: it auto-configures beans based on classpath + properties, provides curated 'starter' dependencies, an embedded server, and production-ready features (Actuator) — so you can go from empty to running service in minutes.

Auto-configuration works via `@Conditional*` — beans are registered only if certain classes/properties are present. That's why adding `spring-boot-starter-data-jpa` automatically wires a DataSource, EntityManager and transaction manager from `application.properties`.
Field injection (`@Autowired` on a field): hard to test, hides dependencies. Use **constructor injection** — immutable, explicit, testable, and the Spring team's official recommendation.
Use `@ConfigurationProperties` classes over scattered `@Value`. Use profiles (`application-prod.yml`) for env-specific config. Enable Actuator + Prometheus/OpenTelemetry from day one.

flashcards.back: Boot adds auto-configuration, starters, embedded server, and Actuator on top of Spring.

quiz.q: The recommended way to inject dependencies is…
quiz.explain: Constructor DI is explicit, immutable, testable.
Beans register only when their conditions match.
Ops-facing endpoints for health, metrics, env, etc.

challenges.text: Build a Hello World REST controller in Spring Boot.
Add JPA with a User entity + repository + a CRUD endpoint.
Add validation (@Valid), global exception handler, and standard error DTO.
Enable Actuator + Prometheus + OpenTelemetry tracing across two services.

interviewQuestions: How do you expose health and metrics?
